req.title: Create observation (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-055 mandates that a clinician can POST a LOINC-coded Observation to /Observation, and that the server rejects payloads missing the 'code' element with HTTP 422. I derive: (1) a positive EP test confirming 201 on a well-formed request; (2) a negative NEG/DT test confirming 422 when 'code' is absent; (3) a boundary BVA test using a minimal-field payload (exactly the required fields, nothing extra) to probe the lower boundary of valid input; (4) a negative DT test for permission denial (non-clinician role); (5) a state-transition STC test verifying that a previously created Observation cannot be re-created with the same identifier (conflict / idempotency boundary). The violation fix: test[4] previously had kind=negative with heuristic=STC, which is invalid because STC is a state-transition coverage heuristic and must be paired with a kind that reflects state exploration — corrected to kind=boundary to properly represent the state-transition probe while preserving the STC heuristic.

tests.name: test_create_observation_valid_loinc_code_returns_201
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource with id <patient_id> exists in fhir-lite
actions: Build a well-formed FHIR Observation payload with resourceType, status, code (LOINC system + code), and subject reference to <patient_id>
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource with id <patient_id> exists in fhir-lite
actions: Build an Observation payload that omits the 'code' element entirely
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing 'code'
trace: R-HLT-055: error pathway – 422 when code missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422(auth_headers, patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers("clinician")
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    body_str = str(body).lower()
    assert "code" in body_str or "operationoutcome" in body_str

tests.name: test_create_observation_minimal_required_fields_boundary
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource with id <patient_id> exists in fhir-lite
actions: Build the smallest valid Observation payload containing exactly and only: resourceType, status, code (LOINC), and subject
POST the minimal payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: HTTP status code is 201 (server accepts minimum viable payload)
trace: BVA lower boundary – fewest possible fields that still satisfy mandatory elements

tests.name: test_create_observation_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: Non-clinician (e.g., 'patient') role credentials are available via <auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource with id <patient_id> exists in fhir-lite
actions: Build a valid FHIR Observation payload with LOINC code
POST the payload to /Observation using non-clinician auth headers
trace: DT rule: role != clinician AND valid payload => DENY

tests.name: test_create_observation_duplicate_identifier_state_transition
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource with id <patient_id> exists in fhir-lite
No Observation with identifier 'obs-idempotency-test-001' pre-exists (cleaned up by <cleanup_observation> fixture)
actions: Build a valid Observation payload with a fixed identifier value 'obs-idempotency-test-001'
POST it once and assert 201 (transition: non-existent -> created)
POST the identical payload a second time
Assert the second response signals a conflict (409) or idempotent 200/201 per server policy
expected: Second POST returns 409 (conflict) OR 200/201 if server is idempotent
Server does not silently create a duplicate resource
trace: R-HLT-055: negative path – conflicting state transitions
STC: state non-existent -> created -> conflict/idempotent on re-create
